core_assumptions: Market discounts everything — all fundamentals are already priced into the chart
Prices move in trends — most technical strategies are built on this
History repeats itself — market participants react consistently to similar stimuli
Charts and graphs can predict changes in demand and supply forces

dow_theory.description: Stock market is not random; guided by specific trends

trend_types.primary: Major trend lasting less than a year to several years — can be bullish or bearish
trend_types.secondary: Intermediate corrections within the primary trend
trend_types.minor: Day-to-day movements lasting only a few days

elliot_wave.description: Long-term major patterns consist of five successive waves

weaknesses: Requires significant experience to identify and interpret patterns correctly
Must identify patterns quickly before opportunity passes
Not suitable for new listings with no price history
Cannot forecast unprecedented events (e.g. 2008 financial crisis)

smart_money_concept.description: Theory that financial markets are largely controlled by institutions, hedge funds, and governments who leave footprints on charts
smart_money_concept.core_idea: Follow smart money — identify where institutions are entering and exit with them
smart_money_concept.market_makers: Banks, hedge funds, and large institutions can move substantial capital and allegedly set traps for retail traders
smart_money_concept.retail_trap: Retail traders fall victim to unexpected swings because they ignore institutional footprints

chart_types.bar_chart: Series of vertical lines showing high, low, open, and close for each period
chart_types.line_chart: Connects closing prices over time; does not show high/low/open

candlestick_chart.description: Shows open, high, low, close with colored bodies
candlestick_chart.bullish_candle: White or clear body — price closed above open
candlestick_chart.bearish_candle: Red or black body — price closed below open

chart_types.point_and_figure: Uses X for rising prices and O for falling prices; not time-based

trend_identification.uptrend: Series of higher highs and higher lows
trend_identification.downtrend: Series of lower highs and lower lows
trend_identification.break_of_structure_BOS: Price sets a new high or low while breaking through former ones — signals trend continuation
trend_identification.change_of_character_CHOCH: Sudden shift in volatility, volume, or price action suggesting trend weakness and possible reversal
trend_identification.market_structure_shift_MSS: In an uptrend: a lower low forms. In a downtrend: a higher high forms. Usually follows displacement

support_and_resistance.support: Price level where demand is strong enough to prevent further decline; buyers step in as price cheapens
support_and_resistance.resistance: Price level where selling pressure prevents further advance; sellers increase and buyers decrease
support_and_resistance.role_reversal: A broken resistance level becomes new support, and vice versa

liquidity.buy_side_liquidity: Area on chart where short-sellers have placed stop orders (above swing highs)
liquidity.sell_side_liquidity: Area on chart where bullish traders have placed stop orders (below swing lows)
liquidity.zones_location: Typically found at extremes of price volatility — tops and bottoms of price patterns

displacement.description: Sudden, forceful price movement — appears as consecutive long candles with minimal wicks in one direction
outcomes: Almost always results in a Market Structure Shift
Almost always creates a Fair Value Gap

inducement.description: Peaks of mini-counter-trends within a larger trend
inducement.purpose: Smart money stop-loss hunting on lower timeframes
inducement.signal: Once inducement level is reached and extra liquidity enters, price reverses and continues original trend

fair_value_gap_FVG.description: Three-candle pattern with a larger center candle and a gap between its wicks and surrounding candles
fair_value_gap_FVG.significance: FVGs tend to be filled at some point in the future
fair_value_gap_FVG.trading_use: ICT traders set orders anticipating the gap will be filled

optimal_trade_entry_OTE.description: Entry point identified using Fibonacci levels after Inducement → Displacement → MSS → FVG sequence
optimal_trade_entry_OTE.fibonacci_range: 61.8% to 78.6% retracement of an expansion range

balanced_price_range_BPR.description: Two Fair Value Gaps created by two opposite Displacements in quick succession
balanced_price_range_BPR.behavior: Price oscillates between extremes trying to fill both FVGs
balanced_price_range_BPR.resolution: Original trend resumes once BPR limits are breached

order_blocks.description: Candle (or group of candles) where institutional orders were placed before a major move
order_blocks.identification: Often appears as a ranging/consolidation area before a sharp directional move
order_blocks.trading_use: Price returns to order blocks as a point of interest for re-entry

trading_sessions.london_session: Manipulation phase — often sweeps Asian session highs/lows
trading_sessions.new_york_session: True directional move; completes the market cycle

order_blocks_OB.description: Market condition where central banks and institutions accumulate or distribute large quantities of an asset
order_blocks_OB.appearance: Appears as a ranging market on the chart

smart_money_concepts_SMC.breaker_blocks: Order blocks that fail to hold price; institutions intentionally break support/resistance to trigger retail stop-losses
smart_money_concepts_SMC.fair_value_gaps_FVG: Market moves quickly from one price to another leaving a gap — signals significant shift in market sentiment
smart_money_concepts_SMC.break_of_structure_BOS: Price sets new high/low while breaking former ones — trend continuation signal
smart_money_concepts_SMC.change_of_character_CHOCH: Abrupt behavioral shift in the market signaling trend weakness or reversal

liquidity_sweep.definition: Market moves aggressively to trigger large pools of orders (stop losses, pending orders) in specific liquidity zones
liquidity_sweep.purpose: Generates market momentum; large players use it to fill large orders before the real move

liquidity_sweep.intent: Clean out accumulated orders and generate momentum

break_of_structure.intent: Signal trend continuation or change in direction

vs_break_of_structure.key_note: What looks like a sweep on higher timeframe may look like BoS on lower timeframe

where_to_spot: Equal highs and swing highs — sell orders accumulate here
Equal lows and swing lows — buy orders accumulate here
Order blocks and order flow areas

liquidity_grab_vs_sweep.sweep: Broader market movement through a liquidity zone triggering large volumes; can affect significant price range
liquidity_grab_vs_sweep.grab: Specific, short-lived move to trigger orders before reversing; more tactical

entry_conditions.rule: Identify the primary trend using market structure (higher highs/higher lows = uptrend; lower highs/lower lows = downtrend)
Wait for confirmed pattern breakout before entering — a pattern is only complete on actual breakout
Apply confirmation filters (intrabar, multiple closes, time, percentage) before acting on a breakout
For gap plays: wait for throwback after breakout gap — if throwback does not fill gap, place buy entry above high of gap bar (pivot)

exit_conditions.rule: For liquidity sweep longs: target next swing high (next buy-side liquidity pool)
For liquidity sweep shorts: target next swing low (next sell-side liquidity pool)
Set take-profit at structural highs (for longs) or structural lows (for shorts)
Calculate price target for double top/bottom: height from peak to trough projected from breakout point
For Head and Shoulders: target = distance from head to neckline, projected down from neckline break
For triangles: target = height of pattern added to (or subtracted from) breakout price
Exit or tighten stop when gap is violated after gap entry — trend assumption breaks down
Once in SMC trade, target the next structural high (long) or structural low (short)

risk_management.rule: Place stop loss below the order block for long trades (bullish setup)
Place stop loss above the order block for short trades (bearish setup)
Always leave a spread buffer when setting stop loss to mitigate slippage
Use stop-and-reverse orders on false breakouts: enter on breakout, place protective stop outside breakout bar, and entry stop at same level in opposite direction
Use risk-reward ratio when setting take profit — fixed ratios (e.g. 1:2, 1:3) are acceptable
Trading is a game of probability — never expect 100% win rate; cut losses and let winners run
Place protective stops based on: trend line / support-resistance level with filter, or percentage/points filter
Set stop loss for gap entries initially at gap low, then move below pivot low as trade develops

head_and_shoulders_top.description: Three peaks with center (head) higher than two outer peaks (shoulders)
head_and_shoulders_top.signal: Bearish reversal at end of uptrend
head_and_shoulders_top.entry: Break below neckline (line connecting the two troughs between peaks)
head_and_shoulders_top.target: Distance from head to neckline, projected downward from neckline
head_and_shoulders_top.reliability: One of the lowest failure rates of all patterns

inverse_head_and_shoulders.description: Three troughs with center lower than outer troughs
inverse_head_and_shoulders.signal: Bullish reversal at end of downtrend
inverse_head_and_shoulders.target: Distance from neckline to head, projected upward

double_top.description: Two successive peaks at roughly the same price level separated by a trough
double_top.target: Height from peak to trough, subtracted from breakout price

double_bottom.description: Two successive troughs at roughly the same price level separated by a peak
double_bottom.target: Height from trough to peak, added to breakout price

triple_top.description: Three distinct peaks at roughly the same price level
triple_top.entry: Break below the lowest trough or trendline connecting troughs
triple_top.target: Height from highest peak to lowest trough, subtracted from lowest trough

triple_bottom.description: Three distinct troughs at roughly the same price level
triple_bottom.signal: Bullish reversal — best after sustained decline
triple_bottom.entry: Break above highest peak or trendline connecting peaks
triple_bottom.target: Height from peak to trough, added to highest peak

rounding_bottom.description: Gradual U-shaped bottom over long period; also called saucer bottom
rounding_bottom.signal: Long-term bullish reversal; best on weekly charts
rounding_bottom.entry: Breakout above both lips of the cup
rounding_bottom.note: Performance ranks about average for bottom patterns

flag.description: Short consolidation (rectangle/parallelogram) sloping opposite to prior sharp move
flag.entry: Breakout in direction of prior trend
flag.target: Equal to height of the flagpole

pennant.description: Short consolidation in a small triangle shape after a sharp move
pennant.target: Equal to height of the flagpole

cup_and_handle.description: Rounded bottom (not V-shaped) with two lips, followed by a handle (flag-like consolidation)
cup_and_handle.entry: Breakout above both lips from the handle
cup_and_handle.target: Height of right cup lip to bottom of cup, added to breakout price

symmetrical.description: Downward upper trendline + upward lower trendline converging
symmetrical.breakout: Can break either direction; best performance is upward
symmetrical.note: Many false breakouts — always confirm breakout
symmetrical.target: Height of pattern added/subtracted from breakout price

ascending.description: Horizontal upper trendline + upward sloping lower trendline
ascending.target: Height of pattern added/subtracted from breakout price

descending.description: Downward sloping upper trendline + horizontal lower trendline
descending.note: Above-average performance on upside break; retracements common
descending.target: Height of pattern added/subtracted from breakout price

wedge_rising.description: Two trendlines heading same direction upward; price must touch each at least 5 times

wedge_falling.description: Two trendlines heading same direction downward
wedge_falling.breakout: Usually upward — bullish signal after panic/decline

rectangle.description: Trading range with horizontal support and resistance; often has many false breakouts
rectangle.shortfall: A shortfall (price failing to reach opposite side of range) often indicates eventual breakout direction
rectangle.target: Height of range added (upside) or subtracted (downside) from breakout

types.breakaway_gap: Occurs when price breaks out of a congestion area; requires high enthusiasm/volume
types.runaway_gap: Also called measuring gap; occurs mid-trend as traders chase moves; represents panic buying/selling
types.exhaustion_gap: Near end of trend; identified by high volume and large price difference from prior close; signals trend ending

gap_pivot_trade.setup: Wait for throwback after gap; if throwback stops without filling gap, that is the pivot low
gap_pivot_trade.entry: Buy entry above high of the gap bar
gap_pivot_trade.stop: Initially at gap low; move below pivot low

gaps.principle: Gaps generally profitable on breakouts from patterns, trends, support/resistance

candlestick_patterns.general_note: Candlestick patterns are traditionally reversal patterns; best performance after sustained trends

doji.description: Open and close at same price; high and low roughly equidistant
doji.signal: Indecision; possible warning of price change

hammer.description: Small body at top of range with long lower wick; bullish signal
hammer.note: Occurs frequently but below-average performance without confirmation

hanging_man.description: Same shape as hammer but appears at top of trend
hanging_man.note: Actually breaks in either direction randomly with slight upward bias

shooting_star.description: Small body at bottom of range with long upper wick; appears at top

inverted_hammer.description: Small body with long upper wick at bottom of trend

engulfing_bullish.description: Short black body followed by tall white body that completely engulfs it
engulfing_bullish.signal: Very good performance on downward breakout in downward trend

engulfing_bearish.description: Short white body followed by tall black body that completely engulfs it

harami_top.description: Large body followed by small body of opposite color contained within the large body
harami_top.signal: Can break either direction — not reliably a reversal

dark_cloud_cover.description: Second candle opens above first and closes into its body; second is black, first is white

piercing_line.description: Opposite of dark cloud; second candle (white) closes into black first candle body

pipe_bottom.description: Two bars with large ranges at end of major downtrend; second bar closes in upper half
pipe_bottom.signal: Bullish reversal; more reliable on weekly data
pipe_bottom.target: Height of taller bar + distance to shorter bar, added to taller bar

indicators.name: Relative Strength Index (RSI)
indicators.formula: RSI = 100 - 100/(1 + RS), where RS = avg up closes / avg down closes over period
indicators.how_to_use: Momentum oscillator measuring speed and change of price movements; overbought above 70, oversold below 30

indicators.name: Moving Averages
indicators.how_to_use: Average of closing prices over a period; used for trend direction and crossover signals
indicators.crossover_signal: When faster MA crosses above slower MA — bullish; below — bearish

indicators.name: Volume
indicators.how_to_use: Confirms breakouts — high volume on breakout = valid; low volume = suspect. High volume + large price gap near trend end = exhaustion gap

indicators.name: Fibonacci Retracement
indicators.how_to_use: Key levels: 23.6%, 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%, 78.6%. ICT uses 61.8%-78.6% for OTE. Traders place orders at these levels creating self-fulfilling liquidity zones

inside_bar.description: Second bar's range contained entirely within first bar's range
inside_bar.entry: Buy breakout above inside bar close; sell breakdown below inside bar close

narrow_range_NR4.description: Fourth bar has a narrower range than the preceding three bars
narrow_range_NR4.entry: Break above or below the narrow range bar

steps: Step 1: Identify valid single-candle liquidity sweep pattern on HTF (e.g. daily/H4) — price sweeps below prior low (bullish) or above prior high (bearish) with one candle, then next candle must not exceed sweep candle
Step 2: Zoom to LTF (e.g. H1/M15) and wait for CHoCH (Change of Character) confirming market structure shift
Step 3: Set limit order at newly formed order block or FVG, or midpoint of combined zone
Step 4: Stop loss below order block (long) or above order block (short), with spread buffer
Step 5: Take profit at next opposite swing point (swing high for longs, swing low for shorts)
ict_liquidity_sweep_strategy.best_for: Forex, crypto, indices on any timeframe
ict_liquidity_sweep_strategy.win_rate_note: High win rate reported by practitioners; requires patience for correct setups

steps: Step 1: Determine primary trend using market structure (series of HH/HL or LH/LL)
Step 2: Wait for CHoCH to identify trend change, then look for first pullback
Step 3: Identify high-probability order block — one that caused CHoCH, has liquidity below it and FVG above it (for bullish)
Step 4: Place buy limit at order block zone
Step 5: Stop loss just below order block; target = structural high
smc_order_block_strategy.key_insight: Wait for liquidity to be taken before entry — market makers take sell-side liquidity before going up

checklist: 2. Identify tradeable Point of Interest (POI) — bearish or bullish order block
3. Identify liquidity zone that needs to be swept near the POI
4. Wait for sweep of that liquidity
5. Enter from the POI after sweep is confirmed
6. Set stop loss: above OB for short, below OB for long
7. Set take profit: next swing high (long) or next swing low (short)

steps: Step 1: Identify pattern (H&S, double top/bottom, triangle, flag, etc.)
Step 2: Wait for confirmed breakout — do not anticipate
Step 3: Apply confirmation filter (multiple closes, percentage move)
Step 4: Enter on break, or wait for throwback/retracement to pattern boundary
Step 5: Stop loss outside pattern boundary with filter
Step 6: Target = pattern height projected from breakout point
chart_pattern_breakout_strategy.false_breakout_play: Enter on breakout, place stop outside opposite side of bar, and also place stop-and-reverse order — profit whether breakout continues or fails

psychology: Trading always carries risk — losses are part of the game; success requires disciplined execution
Do not see patterns where there are none — human tendency to find patterns is dangerous
Do not stick with original price targets after conditions have changed
Patience is required — ICT/SMC requires waiting for specific market conditions before entry
Do not look backwards — focus on forward-looking analysis
SMC narrows trade opportunities by design — not every price move is tradeable
Greater Fool Theory: never buy at any price assuming someone will pay more — value matters

market_regime.description: Higher highs and higher lows; look for longs at pullbacks to order blocks or support
Lower highs and lower lows; look for shorts at rallies to order blocks or resistance
Price oscillating between support and resistance; ICT calls this a balanced price range; wait for breakout or trade fades near extremes
After a large fast candle (displacement); expect FVG to form and price to retrace; look for OTE entry

notes: Candlestick patterns best after sustained trends in that direction
Retracements after breakout are common — can be used for entry
Wedge patterns have below-average performance — use with caution

name: AI Master Strategy
